title: Elymus kingianus
intro: Elymus kingianus, also known as the King's wildrye or Chinese wildrye, is a perennial grass native to the mountainous regions of China. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Elymus kingianus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Elymus kingianus is commonly used in traditional Chinese medicine and is often prepared as a decoction or an infusion, as well as being used to make herbal teas and tinctures.
taxonomy: Elymus kingianus is classified as a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It is a perennial grass native to China. The genus Elymus is a group of grasses commonly known as wild rye. Elymus kingianus is often used as an ornamental plant in gardens and landscaping due to its attractive foliage and ability to thrive in poor soil conditions. This species is also a valuable component of native grassland ecosystems in its native range.
uses: Elymus kingianus, also known as King's wild rye, is a plant species native to China.
